content.text:
[cron:a1000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000001 pipeline-planner] You are a planning agent in a 3-stage analysis pipeline. Follow these steps exactly using absolute paths.

STEP 1: Run exec with command: ls /home/node/workspace/00_task_input/
If the output is empty, stop immediately and do nothing else.

STEP 2: Run read on /home/node/workspace/00_task_input/task_brief.md
Read the FULL content carefully, including the embedded CSV benchmark data.

STEP 3: Analyze the CSV data. Identify which models succeeded (status=ok) and which failed. Note speed (time_s), tool call counts, and any anomalies (zero-length answers, wrong word counts, excessive iterations).

STEP 4: Write an execution plan to /home/node/workspace/01_planner_out/plan.md
The plan MUST contain exactly these sections:

# Execution Plan

## Data Overview
A short paragraph stating how many models passed, how many failed, and the failure categories you observed.

## Models To Profile
A list of EXACTLY 5 model names selected from the passing models. Choose the most informative mix: include the fastest, the slowest passing model, and any model with anomalies worth investigating.

## Questions Per Profile
List EXACTLY 3 questions the executor must answer for every profiled model:
1. What speed tier is this model (fast under 25s, medium 25-65s, slow over 65s)?
2. Did the model use tools correctly (4 tool calls expected) and complete cleanly?
3. Are there any anomalies in this model's row (empty answer, wrong values, extra iterations)?

## Output Format Instructions
Tell the executor to write one section per model with a level-2 markdown header containing the model name, and a one-line answer per question.

STEP 5: Run exec with command: rm /home/node/workspace/00_task_input/task_brief.md

Do not search the web. Use only exec, read, and write. Do not skip any section of the plan.
Current time: Wednesday, June 10th, 2026 - 12:33 AM (UTC)
Reference UTC: 2026-06-10 00:33 UTC

Return your response as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.
